compositional_truth_check.findings: The registry identifies P1-P4, but the visual data confirms only two distinct pot structures: one yellow (P1) and one black (P3/P4). P2 is not visually distinct from the P3/P4 cluster.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_Yellow_Pot: Present. Appears as a soil-filled vessel with a sensor/marker.
inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Present. Identified by the white rabbit anchor.
inventory_reconciliation.P4_Silver_Guest: Systemic Loss. No distinct foliage detected.
inventory_reconciliation.New_Intervention: Introduction of a white cylindrical sensor/marker in the yellow pot and a metallic spoon/tool near the yellow pot in T-3.

plant_audit.P1: Soil surface appears consistent; no visible sprout. Sensor marker present.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen is minimal; white rabbit anchor is visible. No significant growth observed over the 5-day period.
plant_audit.P4: No visible plant material; soil surface is bare.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistent dark, moist appearance across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: White particulate matter (likely perlite or eggshell fragments) present on the surface of the black pot (P3/P4).

temporal_deltas.T_minus_5_to_T_minus_3: Introduction of a metallic spoon near the yellow pot.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_3_to_T_minus_1: Removal of the metallic spoon; lighting conditions fluctuate, obscuring detail.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_1_to_CURRENT: Return to stable lighting; no structural changes to the plant matter.

visual_health_inference.P3: Stagnant. The Pothos shows no signs of active growth or decline, but remains in a state of low-vitality maintenance.
visual_health_inference.P4: Systemic loss confirmed; no biological activity.

anomalies: Presence of a 'Soil Sensor V2.0' marker in P1.
White rabbit anchor in P3 is the only consistent biological/structural marker.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of stasis. The Pothos (P3) remains the only viable specimen, though it is not showing signs of active development. The other pots (P1, P2, P4) appear to be either dormant or suffering from systemic loss of the original specimens. The environment is controlled, with no signs of pests or fungal outbreaks, but the lack of foliage suggests a need for re-evaluation of the growth medium or light exposure.
